description: Kusto Workbench icon in the Activity Bar with the tooltip visible
altText: Activity bar
cropHints: Crop to the Activity Bar area plus the tooltip panel, roughly the left 300-400px of the window
notes: The tooltip appears on hover. Use the Gherkin click step to hover the icon, then immediately screenshot.

description: VS Code settings page showing the 'Show Activity Bar Icon' checkbox for the extension
altText: Settings
cropHints: Crop to the settings editor area showing the relevant extension setting
notes: After opening settings, type 'kustoWorkbench.showActivityBarIcon' into the search box to filter to the relevant setting.

description: Cluster dropdown open showing the 'Import from .xml file...' option highlighted, with no real connection data visible
altText: Import connections for Kusto for desktop or web
cropHints: Crop to section area: include top+left borders (drag handle), query name row, cluster dropdown with open menu, database dropdown, toolbar. Do NOT show placeholder text at the bottom.
notes: Use DEFAULT profile (no connections = no secrets). After opening editor: type a space + Ctrl+S to clear placeholder text and get grey saved-border. Use __testOpenDropdown('cluster-dropdown') to open the dropdown, then set _focusedIndex=1 to highlight 'Import from .xml file...'. Feature file: features/import-connections.feature

description: Query toolbar with cluster/database dropdowns and the 'Add to favorites' star button with injected tooltip
altText: Add combos of cluster + database to your favorites with a friendly name
cropHints: Crop to section area: include top+left borders, query name row, cluster+database dropdowns, star button with tooltip, and toolbar row. No secrets.
notes: Use DEFAULT profile (no secrets). Tooltip is injected via JS (native title tooltips cannot be triggered programmatically). The evaluate step creates a positioned div styled like a native tooltip next to the star button. Feature file: features/add-to-favorites.feature

description: VS Code Copilot chat input with @Kusto Workbench agent selected, showing a model like Claude Opus
altText: VS Code custom agent that actually works called Kusto Workbench
cropHints: Crop to the Copilot Chat panel area showing the agent mention and model
notes: HARD: Needs a live Copilot model. Open the profile manually, type @Kusto Workbench in the chat input, then use --attach-devhost to screenshot.

description: Activity Bar panel showing the 'Export Agent Skill' card with the 'Export Skill...' button
altText: Export a skill that teaches your own agent how to control Kusto Workbench
cropHints: Crop to the Quick Access panel showing the Export Agent Skill action card
notes: Open the Activity Bar view and scroll to the Export Agent Skill section.

description: Single query section with a datatable KQL query, results table showing Product/TotalSales/AvgSales/Rank, and the Add section buttons at the bottom
altText: Kusto query editor
cropHints: Crop to show the full section: query name row, connection bar, toolbar, editor, run button, results table, and Add section buttons. Include section borders.
notes: Uses kusto-auth to execute the datatable query and show real results. After execution, __testSetDropdownText replaces real cluster/database names with fake placeholders. Window is narrow+tall (850x950) with sidebar closed to give the section full width. Feature file: features/kusto-query-editor.feature

description: SQL section with T-SQL query, autocomplete, and results table
altText: SQL sections
cropHints: Full window or crop to the SQL section area showing the editor, autocomplete, and results
notes: TODO entry — this screenshot is currently inside an HTML comment in README.md (line 86). The comment must be uncommented separately once the screenshot is captured. Do NOT uncomment the README as part of the screenshot workflow.

description: URL section loading a GitHub-hosted CSV (Plotly dataset), showing tabular data with row/column counts
altText: Load .csv data directly from the internet
cropHints: Crop to the URL section showing the loaded CSV data table
notes: Needs a URL section configured with a remote CSV URL. The data must be loaded and visible in the table.

description: Transformation section in 'Calculate' mode, adding a derived column with formula, result table shown
altText: Using data transformation to add a calculated column
cropHints: Crop to the transformation section showing the calculation formula and the result table below it
notes: Needs an existing query with results, plus a transformation section configured to add a calculated column.

description: Chart section in edit mode: Bar chart type selected, X/Y/Legend configured, rendered grouped bar chart visible
altText: Creating a bar chart from a Kusto query
cropHints: Crop to the chart section showing the configuration panel and the rendered bar chart
notes: Needs an existing query with results, plus a chart section configured as a bar chart with X, Y, and Legend set.

description: HTML section rendering a dashboard with bar chart, KPI cards, and interactive elements
altText: HTML based dashboard
cropHints: Crop to the HTML section showing the rendered dashboard
notes: HARD: Needs a pre-built HTML section in a .kqlx file with a dashboard (bar chart, KPI cards). Create or open the file, then screenshot.

description: Query toolbar showing the 'Prettify query' tooltip on the format button
altText: Prettify a Kusto query
cropHints: Crop to the query section toolbar area showing the prettify button with its tooltip
notes: Hover the prettify/format button in the toolbar to show the tooltip, then screenshot immediately. Timing-sensitive — tooltip may fade.

description: Editor with syntax error: red squiggles, tooltip showing error message, error bar with go-to-line link
altText: Debugging a Kusto query with syntax errors
cropHints: Crop to the query section showing the error squiggles, tooltip, and the error bar below the editor
notes: Type a KQL query with a deliberate syntax error. Wait for the diagnostics to appear (red squiggles + error bar). May need to hover the error to show the tooltip.

description: A vs B comparison panel: speed/CPU/memory/extents metrics with checkmarks, optimized query, Accept button
altText: Performance optimization of an existing query
cropHints: Crop to the comparison panel showing the performance metrics and the optimized query
notes: Needs two queries that have been compared via the optimization feature. The comparison panel must be open and showing results.

description: Query toolbar with the 'Share query as link (Azure Data Explorer)' tooltip visible
altText: Share your queries as Azure Data Explorer links
cropHints: Crop to the query section toolbar area showing the share button with its tooltip
notes: Hover the share button in the toolbar to show the tooltip. Timing-sensitive.

description: Cached Values viewer showing auth tokens table with multiple accounts, cluster-to-account associations
altText: Multi-account support
cropHints: Crop to the Cached Values viewer showing the auth tokens and account associations
notes: HARD: Needs the Cached Values viewer with multiple authenticated Microsoft accounts visible. Pre-populate by signing in with multiple accounts in the profile.

description: Connection Manager panel with breadcrumb navigation, function list with expanded function showing description/signature
altText: Connection Manager and Cluster Explorer
cropHints: Crop to the Connection Manager panel showing the schema browser with expanded function details
notes: Open the Connection Manager, navigate to a cluster > database > Functions, and expand a function to show its details.

description: Connection Manager header showing All/Favorites/Leave No Trace tabs, with the Leave No Trace shield button tooltip
altText: Leave No Trace
cropHints: Crop to the Connection Manager header area showing the tabs and the Leave No Trace toggle tooltip
notes: Hover the shield/Leave No Trace button on a cluster row to show the tooltip. This is in the Connection Manager, not the cluster explorer sidebar.

description: WYSIWYG markdown editor (TOAST UI) showcasing rich formatting: headings, bold, italic, lists, tables, code blocks
altText: Markdown editing
cropHints: Crop to the markdown section showing the WYSIWYG editor with diverse rich formatting
notes: Open the query editor and add a markdown section (or open a .kqlx/.mdx file that already has one). The section should show varied formatting to demonstrate the editor's capabilities.

description: Python section with pandas/numpy code, inline output showing pivot tables, growth percentages, summary statistics
altText: Python sections with pandras and np
cropHints: Crop to the Python section showing the code cell and its inline output below
notes: HARD: Needs Python installed locally with pandas and numpy. Write Python code that produces interesting output (pivot tables, stats). Run the code first, then screenshot with the output visible.
